reconciled_ai_data.plea_details: Defendants, along with other unknown wrongdoers, incited and abetted by Thomas Hawe, forcibly entered and held 4 acres of land, meadow, and pasture belonging to Thomas Greswold, against the statute concerning forcible entries. Armed with swords, staves, bows, arrows, axes, helmets, sallets, lances, and bills of war, they also removed Greswold's drainpipe, emptied his pond of water and fish (20 pike, 20 bream, 20 tench, and 200 roach, valued at 100 shillings), cut and stole the pipe's valve (valued at 20 pence). They then proceeded to Greswold's house, a Justice of the Peace, and assaulted him while he was at supper. Despite Greswold identifying himself as a JP and ordering them to keep the peace, they assaulted him again, shooting many arrows and causing a great and terrible affray.
